title: Power Distribution in AI and Beyond - TCR 04/11/26
description:
Five major U.S. offshore wind farms collectively powering over two million homes crossed from legal vulnerability into physical permanence after the Interior Department's final appeal deadline expired - and Sam Altman published a blog post declaring AI must be democratized and power cannot be too concentrated, hours after a Molotov cocktail was thrown at his home.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-11-2026/

Five offshore wind projects representing over 4 gigawatts of generation capacity secured permanent construction rights after federal courts ruled that their permits cannot be arbitrarily suspended, and the government chose not to appeal. Sam Altman responded to the attack on his home with a blog post listing his core beliefs - that AI must be democratized, power cannot be too concentrated, and no AI lab should make the most consequential decisions about humanity's future - while describing AGI as having a "ring of power" quality that demands no one hold the ring. Every principle he articulated runs directly counter to OpenAI's trajectory from open research lab to closed platform, a tension the newsletter examines in depth. Port Washington, Wisconsin and Festus, Missouri became the latest communities to assert democratic control over AI infrastructure siting - one voting 2-to-1 to require ballot approval for data center tax breaks, the other ousting every incumbent council member over a data center deal. A California lawsuit against OpenAI documented the precise structural gap between automated safety detection and institutional response: the company's own system flagged a user for mass-casualty weapons activity, deactivated his account, and a human reviewer restored it within 24 hours. EV transaction prices narrowed to within $5,800 of combustion vehicles, the smallest gap ever recorded. Google moved its post-quantum cryptography readiness deadline from the early 2030s to 2029 after two papers demonstrated major advances in quantum computing capability. University of Chicago researchers identified zeaxanthin, a common plant nutrient, as a direct enhancer of cancer-fighting T cells that improved immunotherapy outcomes in animal studies.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-11-2026/
